skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI video creation agent that produces short videos (5-120 seconds) from natural language descriptions. Supports product ads, social media content, brand videos, explainers, and promotional clips through the Pexo API.
skill.summary: Create short AI-generated videos from natural language descriptions using the Pexo API.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: All 534 static analysis findings have been evaluated and are dismissed as false positives for this legitimate API client CLI tool. The external_commands findings (349) reflect standard shell script patterns in a bash-based CLI tool. The network findings (27) reference the legitimate pexo.ai API endpoints. The filesystem findings (79) access the intended ~/.pexo config directory and standard temp files. The env_access findings (25) read the intentionally required PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL variables. No malicious intent, obfuscation, or prompt injection was detected. The combined capabilities warrant MEDIUM risk classification due to elevated operational risk, but the skill is safe to publish with appropriate warnings.

medium_findings.title: Broad system access required for operation
medium_findings.description: This skill requires external command execution, network access, filesystem access, and environment variable access to function as an API client for the Pexo video creation service. All script calls target the legitimate pexo.ai API. This combination of capabilities is standard for CLI tooling but represents elevated operational risk that users should be aware of.

medium_findings.title: Shell script invocations with user-provided parameters
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes shell scripts with variable interpolation from user-provided project IDs, file paths, and messages. While the scripts use set -euo pipefail and validate inputs (e.g., file existence checks, asset tag validation), command injection via crafted file paths or project IDs remains a theoretical risk.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM database reference (False Positive - Dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:43 as containing a Windows SAM database reference. The actual content is a language compliance instruction: 'You MUST reply to the user in the SAME language they use.' This is a clear false positive with no security implications.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm references in documentation (False Positive - Dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in SKILL.md lines 3, 90, 112, 261, 331. These lines contain standard skill metadata (base64-encoded description), documentation about Pexo's video production process, and references to OSS URL parameters (Signature/OssAccessKeyId). No cryptographic code exists in these files.

low_findings.title: Hidden file access - ~/.pexo/config (False Positive - Dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged access to ~/.pexo/config and related hidden files as a HIGH filesystem risk. The ~/.pexo/config file is the intended configuration location for this CLI tool, mimicking Kubernetes(~/.kube/config) and other CLI tools. Storing API keys in ~/.pexo/config is the documented design pattern.

low_findings.title: Dangerous combination heuristic (False Positive - Dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a heuristic dangerous combination of 'Code execution + Network + Credential access'. This is the expected behavior for an API client CLI tool. The code is open source, well-structured, uses set -euo pipefail, and only communicates with the legitimate pexo.ai API. No obfuscation or data exfiltration was detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: API key transmitted as Bearer token in HTTP headers
dangerous_patterns.description: The PEXO_API_KEY environment variable is read and passed directly in HTTP Authorization headers via curl commands. This is standard for REST API authentication. The secret is masked in diagnostic output by pexo-doctor.sh (mask_secret function). Users should ensure the API key is stored securely and not shared.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command substitution with variable interpolation in API calls
dangerous_patterns.description: Shell scripts use command substitution and variable interpolation to construct API request URLs, request bodies, and file paths (e.g., ${PEXO_BASE_URL}${path}, "$filepath", '{"file_name":"$filename"}'). Standard bash pattern but requires trust that the Pexo API endpoint responds as expected.

dangerous_patterns.title: File upload with direct file path access
dangerous_patterns.description: The pexo-upload.sh script reads local files specified by the user and uploads them to Pexo's storage via HTTP PUT. File path validation checks exist (file existence, MIME type, file size), but file content is sent to a third-party service. Users should only upload files they are authorized to share.

content.user_title: Create AI Short Videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Creating short-form video content through a chat interface is complex without the right tools. Pexo Agent bridges this gap by letting you describe your video in natural language while it handles scriptwriting, shot composition, and production through the Pexo API.
actual_capabilities: Create short videos from 5 to 120 seconds using natural language descriptions
Upload and reference images, videos, and audio files as source material
Poll for video production status and deliver completed video files and URLs
Support revisions and preview selection for iterative video refinement
Handle credit management and error recovery automatically
Support 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 aspect ratios for different platforms
limitations: Cannot generate videos longer than 120 seconds
Requires a valid PEXO_API_KEY from pexo.ai and internet connectivity
Video production takes 15 to 20 minutes minimum, not real-time
Cannot crawl web URLs - files must be downloaded to the local machine before upload

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: Create TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts by describing your concept in natural language. Upload reference images and let Pexo handle the production.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Generate professional product advertisements and brand videos by providing product images and a brief description of your marketing message.

use_cases.title: Explainer and Educational Content
use_cases.description: Produce short explainer videos and educational clips by describing the topic and key points you want to cover.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Video Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for my new running shoes.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a basic video

prompt_templates.title: Video with Reference Asset
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a promotional video for my coffee shop. Here is our logo image. Make it warm and inviting.
prompt_templates.scenario: User uploading a reference image for brand consistency

prompt_templates.title: Revision and Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: The previous video is good but make the text overlay larger and use a more energetic background music track.
prompt_templates.scenario: User providing feedback to revise an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Asset Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a before-and-after transformation video for our home renovation service. Here are the before photos and after photos.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating a complex video with multiple reference files

output_examples.input: Create a 30-second ad for my bakery showing our new croissant selection.
output_examples.output: Video submitted to Pexo. Production time: approximately 15 to 20 minutes. You will receive the download link and video file when ready.

output_examples.input: Make the video shorter and add more close-up shots of the food.
output_examples.output: Revision sent to Pexo. Your feedback has been submitted for the existing project. Waiting for the updated video.

output_examples.input: Create a before-and-after video for our home renovation portfolio.
output_examples.output: Project created. Uploading before and after photos. Request submitted to Pexo. Estimated completion in 15 to 20 minutes.

best_practices: Provide clear, specific descriptions of your desired video content. Pexo works best when the concept is well-defined and focused.
Upload reference images whenever possible to ensure brand consistency and visual alignment with your vision.
Wait at least 60 seconds between status checks during production to avoid unnecessary API calls and rate limits.
anti_patterns: Do not add technical video parameters (duration, aspect ratio, style) that the user did not specify. Pexo handles these automatically.
Do not rephrase or summarize the user's message when sending it to Pexo. Copy it exactly as written for best results.
Do not make multiple API calls in parallel. Pexo processes requests sequentially and concurrent calls may cause errors.

faq.question: What types of videos can Pexo create?
faq.answer: Pexo can create short videos from 5 to 120 seconds in 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 aspect ratios. This includes product ads, social media content, brand videos, explainers, and promotional clips.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A typical 15-second video takes about 15 to 20 minutes to produce. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for upload?
faq.answer: Supported formats include images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: Do I need a Pexo account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a PEXO_API_KEY from pexo.ai. The skill uses this key to authenticate with the Pexo API for video production.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. You can send revision requests for existing projects. Pexo will process your feedback and produce an updated version.

faq.question: How do I check my available credits?
faq.answer: Credit balance is checked automatically when credit-related errors occur. You can also check manually at pexo.ai under the Credits section.
